arts.ttl: How to watch Leicester City vs Brighton - TV channel, live stream details and kick-off time - Leicestershire Live
arts.sum: Leicester City will look to make it two wins from two under new manager Ruud van Nistelrooy on Sunday following a perfect start to life under the Dutchman. The match is being played at the King Power Stadium, the second home game for Leicester of the week.

arts.ttl: Leicester vs Brighton Preview: Team News, Predicted Lineups and Score Prediction
arts.sum: Leicester will look to follow up their midweek win over West Ham with another three points when they welcome Brighton to the King Power in the Premier League today. Ahead of the clash, we’ve...

arts.ttl: Odsonne Edouard the unwanted as Celtic hero brutally cut off by 2 unimpressed managers amid looming transfer exit
arts.sum: The latest hammer blow for Edouard comes days after he was publicly dismissed by Oliver Glasner

arts.ttl: Storm Darragh chaos hands Jamie Vardy Leicester City hope amid Premier League chase
arts.sum: When Mohamed Salah scored for the seventh game in a row at Newcastle on Wednesday, fears of realisation creeped in that Liverpool forward could, in fact, match Jamie Vardy's record against the Foxes at Anfield on Boxing Day. Salah now can only get to scoring in nine successive Premier League matches before the Boxing Day visit of Van Nistelrooy's Foxes.

arts.ttl: Ruud van Nistelrooy's Jamie Vardy verdict hints at Leicester City captain's future
arts.sum: Jamie Vardy has the DNA to keep defying his age, Ruud van Nistelrooy has said, with the Leicester City manager brushing off suggestions the striker might need resting. “The way he is training and performing there is no need to look at resting him at this moment,” van Nistelrooy said.

arts.ttl: Player will ask to leave Brighton in January – Wants to remain in the Premier League
arts.sum: Brighton and Hove Albion midfielder Julio Enciso will ask to leave the club in January and go to another Premier League team. That’s according to journalist Christian Martin, with the information from him relayed by La Nacion. The newspaper explains that Martin was recently on Versus radio where he was discussing Enciso’s immediate future. arts.ttl: Evan Ferguson to Leicester verdict delivered - January transfer, Eduoard decision, loan dilemma - Jordan Blackwell - Leicestershire Live
arts.sum: Leicester, who appointed Ruud van Nistelrooy as their new manager to replace Steve Cooper last month, could do with an injection of quality in attack in January and they are chasing a permanent deal for Brighton's out-of-favour striker Ferguson, according to The Sun. City think that the 20-year-old would be ideal for their side as they battle to stay in the top flight. If City are going to sign a new striker, it has to be somebody with something different to offer and Ferguson certainly ticks that box.

arts.ttl: Team News: Leicester City vs. Brighton & Hove Albion injury, suspension list, predicted XIs
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest injury and suspension news ahead of Sunday's Premier League clash between Leicester City and Brighton & Hove Albion.

arts.ttl: Leicester boss Ruud van Nistelrooy wants to 'bring in Man United starlet on loan in the January transfer window'
arts.sum: Van Nistelrooy enjoyed a winning start as Leicester manager as he guided the Foxes to a 3-1 win at home to West Ham.

arts.ttl: ‘We spoke about that…’: van Nistelrooy shares Leicester transfer plan for January
arts.sum: Leicester City manager Ruud van Nistelrooy has confirmed that he will have funds to strengthen the squad during the January transfer window. When asked if funds will be made available in January, he said to Leicester Mercury: “Yes”.
